mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding real economic activity, raising corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for INDPRO against 60-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or the mean return difference between high-INDPRO-growth and low-INDPRO-growth periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 5.0%, it signals a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 5.0%, the 5-day SPX return is negative more than 60% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) confirm robust consumer demand recovery, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the Sharpe ratio of a trading strategy long SPX when both PAYEMS and RSAFS rise month-over-month is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or if the cross-term coefficient in a regression of forward returns on the joint indicator is not positive and significant (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or near zero, monetary policy is maximally accommodative, compressing discount rates and providing ongoing support for equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million units annualized signals strengthening residential construction and consumer confidence, leading to positive 5-day S&P 500 returns as the market reprices growth expectations.
failure_condition: After HOUST triggers above 1200, the 5-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of low federal funds rates and rising industrial production amplifies positive equity returns: low rates reduce discount rates while rising production improves earnings outlook, together providing a strong boost to S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If the combined cross-term (change in INDPRO multiplied by negative of FEDFUNDS) does not have a positive and statistically significant coefficient (p < 0.10) in a regression of 20-day forward SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase from a recession, strong GDP growth (GDPC1) is especially powerful in driving positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon, as it confirms the durability of the rebound.
failure_condition: During NBER recovery periods, the correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 60-day SPX return is not positive and significant (p < 0.10) over multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year, it erodes real purchasing power and triggers expectations of monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: After CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, the 20-day SPX return is positive more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is not negative.
